chemaxon.metabolism

Class Metabolite

  • java.lang.Object
    • chemaxon.metabolism.Metabolite

  • public class Metabolite
extends Object
    A Metabolite object represents an item in a metabolic tree. It stores references to the corresponding molecule object, to its parent, children, and duplicates, and calculated indicator values.

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      static class  Metabolite.Likelihood
      Likelihood define probability categories.
      static class  Metabolite.State
      State distinguishes active, passive, dormant, and extinct metabolites.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Metabolite(Molecule molecule, Metabolite parent, String reactionID, int phase)
      Creates a new Metabolite instance.

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      double getAccumulation()
      Gets the accumulation indicator of the metabolite.
      Metabolite getChild(int index)
      Gets a direct child metabolite of this one.
      int getChildCount()
      Gets the number of direct child metabolites.
      int getChildCount(Metabolite.State state)
      Gets the number of direct child metabolites being in the given state.
      int getDegradation()
      Gets the degradation rate of the metabolite.
      Metabolite getDuplicate(int index)
      Gets a reference to a duplicate metabolite.
      int getDuplicateCount()
      Gets the number of registered duplicate metabolites of this one.
      int getFormation()
      Gets the formation rate of the metabolite.
      int getGeneration()
      Gets the generation of the metabolite which is the number of transformations steps in its formation route from the substrate.
      double getGlobalAccumulation()
      Gets the global accumulation indicator of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways.
      int getGlobalFormation()
      Gets the global formation rate of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways.
      double getGlobalProduction()
      Gets the global production indicator of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways.
      String getKey()
      Gets a SMILES string as a unique molecule identifier key.
      Metabolite.Likelihood getLikelihood()
      Gets the probability of being a major metabolite.
      Molecule getMolecule()
      Gets the Molecule object of the metabolite.
      Metabolite getParent()
      Gets a reference to the precursor of the metabolite, the metabolite from which this metabolite was directly formed.
      int getPhase()
      Gets the phase of the metabolite which is the phase class of its direct formation reaction.
      double getProduction()
      Gets the production indicator of the metabolite.
      String getReactionID()
      Gets the identifier of the formation reaction of the metabolite from its parent.
      String getRoute()
      Gets a comma separated list or reaction identifiers representing the formation route of the metabolite.
      String getRoutes()
      Gets a semicolon separated list of reaction routes representing the alternative formation pathways of the compound.
      int getSensitivity(int atomIndex)
      Gets the metabolic sensitivity of the given atom in degradation rate.
      Metabolite.State getState()
      Gets the state of a metabolite.
      double getTransmissivity()
      Gets the transmissivity indicator of the metabolite.
      boolean hasParent(Molecule molecule)
      Looks up for a parent having the given molecular structure along the formation route of the metabolite.
      boolean isEnabled()
      Determines if a metabolite is enabled or not.
      boolean isMinor()
      Determines if a metabolite is tagged as minor or not.
      static String percentage(double value, int decimals)
      Converts the given number to a percentage value with the given number of decimal digits, negative means no rounding.
      boolean registerDuplicate(Metabolite duplicate)
      Stores a reference to a duplicate metabolite.
      void setAccumulation(double value)
      Sets the accumulation indicator of the metabolite.
      void setDegradation(int value)
      Sets the degradation rate of the metabolite.
      void setEnabled(boolean value)
      Enables or disables an active metabolite and its duplicates.
      void setFormation(int value)
      Sets the formation rate of the metabolite.
      void setGlobalAccumulation(double value)
      Sets the global accumulation of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways.
      void setGlobalFormation(int value)
      Sets the global formation rate of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways.
      void setGlobalProduction(double value)
      Sets the global production indicator of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways.
      void setLikelihood(Metabolite.Likelihood value)
      Sets the probability of being a major metabolite.
      void setProduction(double value)
      Sets the production indicator of the metabolite.
      void setSensitivity(int atomIndex, int value)
      Sets the metabolic sensitivity of the given atom in degradation rate.
      void setState(Metabolite.State value)
      Gets the state of a metabolite.
      void setTransmissivity(double value)
      Sets the transmissivity indicator of the metabolite.
      void sortChildren(Comparator<Metabolite> comparator)
      Sorts the child metabolites using the given comparator.
      Molecule toMolecule(int decimals)
      Converts the metabolite to a Molecule object having its calculated values stored as properties.
      RxnMolecule toReaction(int decimals)
      Converts the metabolite to a formation scheme as an RxnMolecule object having its calculated values stored as properties.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • Metabolite

        public Metabolite(Molecule molecule,
          Metabolite parent,
          String reactionID,
          int phase)
        Creates a new Metabolite instance.
        Parameters:
        molecule - the Molecule object
        parent - the parent compound of the new metabolite, null for the substrate
        reactionID - the identifier of the formation reaction of the metabolite from its parent, null for the substrate
        phase - the phase of the metabolite which is the phase class of its direct formation reaction

    • Method Detail

      • getAccumulation

        public double getAccumulation()
        Gets the accumulation indicator of the metabolite. Accumulation is a relative, normalized number representing the amount of the original substance that is formed to the current metabolite but not converted further. Metabolites produced quickly and consumed slowly accumulate.
        Returns:
        the accumulation indicator value
        See Also:
        getGlobalAccumulation()

      • getChild

        public Metabolite getChild(int index)
        Gets a direct child metabolite of this one.
        Parameters:
        index - the index of the child metabolite
        Returns:
        a child metabolite
        See Also:
        getChildCount(), getChildCount(State)

      • getChildCount

        public int getChildCount()
        Gets the number of direct child metabolites.
        Returns:
        the number of children
        See Also:
        getChild(int), getChildCount(State)

      • getChildCount

        public int getChildCount(Metabolite.State state)
        Gets the number of direct child metabolites being in the given state.
        Parameters:
        state - the state of the child metabolites to count.
        Returns:
        the number of children
        See Also:
        getChildCount()

      • getDegradation

        public int getDegradation()
        Gets the degradation rate of the metabolite.
        Returns:
        the degradation rate
        See Also:
        getFormation()

      • getDuplicate

        public Metabolite getDuplicate(int index)
        Gets a reference to a duplicate metabolite. Duplicates have the same molecular structure but formed in different routes.
        Parameters:
        index - the index of the duplicate
        Returns:
        a duplicate metabolite
        See Also:
        getDuplicateCount(), registerDuplicate(Metabolite)

      • getDuplicateCount

        public int getDuplicateCount()
        Gets the number of registered duplicate metabolites of this one. Duplicates have the same molecular structure but formed in different routes.
        Returns:
        the number of duplicates
        See Also:
        getDuplicate(int), registerDuplicate(Metabolite)

      • getFormation

        public int getFormation()
        Gets the formation rate of the metabolite.
        Returns:
        the formation rate
        See Also:
        getDegradation()

      • getGeneration

        public int getGeneration()
        Gets the generation of the metabolite which is the number of transformations steps in its formation route from the substrate. The generation of the substrate is zero.
        Returns:
        the Molecule object of the metabolite.

      • getGlobalAccumulation

        public double getGlobalAccumulation()
        Gets the global accumulation indicator of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways. Accumulation is a relative, normalized number representing the amount of the original substance that is formed to the current metabolite but not converted further. Metabolites produced quickly and consumed slowly accumulate. Metabolites having the highest global accumulation values are considered major metabolites.
        Returns:
        the global accumulation indicator value
        See Also:
        getAccumulation()

      • getGlobalFormation

        public int getGlobalFormation()
        Gets the global formation rate of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways.
        Returns:
        the global formation rate
        See Also:
        getFormation()

      • getGlobalProduction

        public double getGlobalProduction()
        Gets the global production indicator of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways. Production is a relative, normalized number representing the amount of the original substrate transformed to the current metabolite.
        Returns:
        the global production indicator
        See Also:
        getProduction(), getGlobalAccumulation()

      • getKey

        public String getKey()
              throws IOException
        Gets a SMILES string as a unique molecule identifier key.
        Returns:
        the identifier string
        Throws:
        IOException

      • getLikelihood

        public Metabolite.Likelihood getLikelihood()
        Gets the probability of being a major metabolite. Major metabolites have the highest accumulation values.
        Returns:
        the probability of formation.

      • getMolecule

        public Molecule getMolecule()
        Gets the Molecule object of the metabolite.
        Returns:
        the Molecule object of the metabolite

      • getParent

        public Metabolite getParent()
        Gets a reference to the precursor of the metabolite, the metabolite from which this metabolite was directly formed.
        Returns:
        the parent metabolite
        See Also:
        getChild(int)

      • getPhase

        public int getPhase()
        Gets the phase of the metabolite which is the phase class of its direct formation reaction.
        Returns:
        the phase value

      • getProduction

        public double getProduction()
        Gets the production indicator of the metabolite. Production is a relative, normalized number representing the amount of the original substrate transformed to the current metabolite.
        Returns:
        the production indicator value
        See Also:
        getGlobalProduction()

      • getReactionID

        public String getReactionID()
        Gets the identifier of the formation reaction of the metabolite from its parent.
        Returns:
        the routes to the metabolite
        See Also:
        getRoute()

      • getRoute

        public String getRoute()
        Gets a comma separated list or reaction identifiers representing the formation route of the metabolite.
        Returns:
        the route string
        See Also:
        getRoutes()

      • getRoutes

        public String getRoutes()
        Gets a semicolon separated list of reaction routes representing the alternative formation pathways of the compound.
        Returns:
        the route string
        See Also:
        getRoute()

      • getSensitivity

        public int getSensitivity(int atomIndex)
        Gets the metabolic sensitivity of the given atom in degradation rate.
        Parameters:
        atomIndex - zero-based index of an atom
        Returns:
        the sensitivity value

      • getState

        public Metabolite.State getState()
        Gets the state of a metabolite. A metabolite becomes active when its child metabolites have been enumerated and its indicators are calculated. Dormant and extinct metabolites are enumerated for the calculation of their parents. Dormant metabolites are enqueued and waiting for further processing while extinct ones are never processed further, in accordance with the actual constraints, due to low formation, for example.
        Returns:
        the state of the metabolite

      • getTransmissivity

        public double getTransmissivity()
        Gets the transmissivity indicator of the metabolite. Transmissivity is a number betwen zero and one indicating the ratio of the degradation and formation rates.
        Returns:
        the transmissivity indicator
        See Also:
        getFormation(), getDegradation()

      • isEnabled

        public boolean isEnabled()
        Determines if a metabolite is enabled or not. Disabled metabolites are ignored during indicator calculations.
        Returns:
        value true when the metabolite is enabled, false otherwise

      • hasParent

        public boolean hasParent(Molecule molecule)
                  throws IOException
        Looks up for a parent having the given molecular structure along the formation route of the metabolite.
        Parameters:
        molecule - a chemical compound
        Returns:
        true if the given compound is a parent of this metabolite, false otherwise
        Throws:
        IOException
        See Also:
        hasParent(String)

      • isMinor

        public boolean isMinor()
        Determines if a metabolite is tagged as minor or not. Metabolites are tagged minor if they are considered unpreferred by the selectivity rule of their formation reaction in the biotransformation library.
        Returns:
        true if the metabolite is tagged as minor, otherwise false

      • percentage

        public static String percentage(double value,
                int decimals)
        Converts the given number to a percentage value with the given number of decimal digits, negative means no rounding.
        Parameters:
        value - the number to convert to percentage
        decimals - the number of decimal digits, negative means no limit
        Returns:
        the value converted to percentage

      • registerDuplicate

        public boolean registerDuplicate(Metabolite duplicate)
                          throws MetabolizerException
        Stores a reference to a duplicate metabolite. Duplicate metabolites having the same molecular structure but formed in different routes.
        Parameters:
        duplicate - the duplicate metabolite
        Returns:
        true
        Throws:
        MetabolizerException
        See Also:
        getDuplicate(int)

      • setAccumulation

        public void setAccumulation(double value)
        Sets the accumulation indicator of the metabolite. Accumulation is a relative, normalized number representing the amount of the original substance that is formed to the current metabolite but not converted further. Metabolites produced quickly and consumed slowly accumulate.
        Parameters:
        value - the accumulation indicator
        See Also:
        setProduction(double)

      • setDegradation

        public void setDegradation(int value)
        Sets the degradation rate of the metabolite.
        Parameters:
        value - the degradation rate
        See Also:
        setFormation(int)

      • setEnabled

        public void setEnabled(boolean value)
        Enables or disables an active metabolite and its duplicates. Disabled metabolites are not considered in the calculations. Disabling a metabolite disables all of its active child metabolites as well. Enabling a metabolite enables its disabled parents. Do not forget to recalculate indicators and globals after changing the enabled/disabled property of a metabolite.
        Parameters:
        value - true to enable a metabolite, false otherwise

      • setFormation

        public void setFormation(int value)
        Sets the formation rate of the metabolite.
        Parameters:
        value - the formation rate
        See Also:
        setGlobalFormation(int), setDegradation(int)

      • setGlobalAccumulation

        public void setGlobalAccumulation(double value)
        Sets the global accumulation of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways. Accumulation is a relative, normalized number representing the amount of the original substance that is formed to the current metabolite but not converted further. Metabolites produced quickly and consumed slowly accumulate.
        Parameters:
        value - the formation rate
        See Also:
        setAccumulation(double), setGlobalProduction(double)

      • setGlobalFormation

        public void setGlobalFormation(int value)
        Sets the global formation rate of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways.
        Parameters:
        value - the formation rate
        See Also:
        setFormation(int)

      • setGlobalProduction

        public void setGlobalProduction(double value)
        Sets the global production indicator of the metabolite which considers all alternative pathways. Production is a relative, normalized number representing the amount of the original substrate transformed to the current metabolite.
        Parameters:
        value - the global production rate
        See Also:
        setProduction(double), setGlobalAccumulation(double)

      • setProduction

        public void setProduction(double value)
        Sets the production indicator of the metabolite. Production is a relative, normalized number representing the amount of the original substrate transformed to the current metabolite.
        Parameters:
        value - the production indicator
        See Also:
        setGlobalProduction(double), setAccumulation(double)

      • setLikelihood

        public void setLikelihood(Metabolite.Likelihood value)
        Sets the probability of being a major metabolite. Major metabolites have the highest accumulation values.
        Parameters:
        value - the new likelihood value.

      • setSensitivity

        public void setSensitivity(int atomIndex,
                  int value)
        Sets the metabolic sensitivity of the given atom in degradation rate.
        Parameters:
        atomIndex - zero-based index of an atom
        value - the new sensitivity value

      • setState

        public void setState(Metabolite.State value)
        Gets the state of a metabolite. A metabolite becomes active when its child metabolites have been enumerated and its indicators are calculated. Dormant and extinct metabolites are enumerated for the calculation of their parents. Dormant metabolites are enqueued and waiting for further processing while extinct ones are never processed further, in accordance with the actual constraints, due to low formation, for example.
        Parameters:
        value - the new state of the metabolite

      • setTransmissivity

        public void setTransmissivity(double value)
        Sets the transmissivity indicator of the metabolite. Transmissivity is a number betwen zero and one indicating the ratio of the degradation and formation rates.
        Parameters:
        value - the production indicator

      • sortChildren

        public void sortChildren(Comparator<Metabolite> comparator)
        Sorts the child metabolites using the given comparator.
        Parameters:
        comparator - a metabolite comparator for the sorting

      • toMolecule

        public Molecule toMolecule(int decimals)
        Converts the metabolite to a Molecule object having its calculated values stored as properties.
        Parameters:
        decimals - indicators are stored as percentage with the given number of decimal digits, negative means no limit
        Returns:
        the metabolite molecule with the calculated properties.
        See Also:
        toReaction(int)

      • toReaction

        public RxnMolecule toReaction(int decimals)
        Converts the metabolite to a formation scheme as an RxnMolecule object having its calculated values stored as properties.
        Parameters:
        decimals - indicators are stored as percentage with the given number of decimal decimal digits, negative means no limit
        Returns:
        the formation reaction with the calculated properties.
        See Also:
        toMolecule(int)
